results.claim: Monitor warned that standalone GDN recurrence kernel at 1.53x speedup only yields 0.98% deflated E2E, below the 1% threshold
Roofline analysis proved standalone kernel is definitively sub-threshold: even at 92% BW (795 GB/s), only 0.88% deflated E2E
Monitor corrected champion's f_decode from 6.96% to 6.34% because z-norm is outside the custom op
Tier 2 benchmark showed only 1.11x speedup vs champion's 1.53x claim in proposal
ncu evidence shows 3x occupancy increase yields only 11% BW improvement; 85% BW is not achievable
Champion-2 computed 1055 GB/s exceeding L40S theoretical peak of 864 GB/s, and in_proj_ba pivot carries torch.compile graph-change risk like Round 1 op008 failure
Delegate found existing fused GDN decode kernel (op013) in git history; champion proceeding to write proposal without reading delegate results
Monitor sent proactive warning about warm-cache inflation before champion received Triton micro-experiment results showing impossible BW numbers (2362 GB/s vs L40S 864 GB/s peak)
Champion claimed 4-6 us savings from kernel launch overhead under CUDA graphs, but CUDA graph replay has near-zero launch overhead; realistic savings only 3.2 us from fusion
BV=8 pipeline data was 0.97x SLOWER (negative), yet champion was interpolating to 1.12x production claim; monitor flagged as unsupported
Gate 5.2 revealed massive discrepancy: gate_up_proj 1.337x vs micro-experiment 2.981x, down_proj 0.99x vs 2.144x
Same-day baseline 7.742s essentially identical to opt 7.743s, suggesting 0% real improvement; 1.06% claim relies on cross-session Stage 1 baseline (7.825s)
Champion ran --overwrite twice, first deleting Stage 1 baseline, then deleting freshly-captured golden_refs
Monitor warned that champion's static max-autotune Triton test shows bit-exactness, but vLLM production uses Inductor default mode which may use different kernels
Champion used hardcoded CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 instead of gpu_reservation.py
Monitor used champion's own roofline output to redirect strategy from standalone to fused approach
Monitor used champion's own ncu data (3x occupancy, only 11% BW gain) to prove 85% BW target unachievable
Monitor's CRITICAL flag caused champion to remove unsupported interpolation, correct all scenarios to below-threshold, and add 'honest assessment' section
Champion-3 identified their benchmark was wrong via INPLACE_FINAL_STATE mismatch, correctly deferred to champion-1
Champion ran test_inductor_default.py confirming production compilation path diverges identically to eager cuBLAS, and explicitly acknowledged monitor's warning
Independent validator tested 7 correctness scenarios with random seeds, edge cases, and CUDA graph replay, all bit-exact
Independent validator ran 32/32 dispatch-range tests with independent 240 MB L2 flush methodology
Validator's independent kernel benchmark directly contradicted champion's debate claims, revealing cold-cache inflation
Validator independently tested M-sweep correctness and confirmed bit-exact for M<=12, 1-ULP divergence for M>=13 (cuBLAS tiling root cause)
FP8 op007 failed Gate 5.1b re-eval: 89.0% vs 89.5% baseline, lost 4 questions gained 3, net -1
Op008 failed Gate 5.1b: 88.0% vs 89.5% baseline, lost 3 questions due to cuBLAS tiling divergence
Op009 failed kill gate: ncu BW 604 GB/s at 69.9% of peak, pipeline 47.06 us vs Triton 46.42 us (1.4% slower)
Op012 failed Gate 5.1b: 88.5% vs 89.0%, same cuBLAS tiling divergence class as op008
Campaign exhausted after 5 rounds with 5.1% cumulative lossless E2E improvement
Debate projected 7.24% deflated E2E for op007 with floor of 2.40%, but actual was 1.06%
Bottleneck analysis verdict of 'PASS - No red flags' lumped 64.5% BW (down_proj) with 92.2% BW (gate_up_proj), concealing optimization headroom
gate_up_proj at 37% f_decode and 92.2% BW utilization is essentially at the hardware ceiling with only 1.08x per-kernel ceiling
op011 exceeded projection by 3x: 3.95% measured vs 1.30% deflated projection
Campaign landscape confirmed exhausted by 2 independent R5 debate champions
All 3 champions independently proposed GDN merge, demonstrating strong convergence
Shape-dependent conditional was dead code during torch.compile tracing; fix was to remove shape dependency entirely
Micro-experiment reported 30.87 us but production pipeline measured 46.42 us -- 1.5x discrepancy from L2 write-back overhead
DAG identified 1 trajectory-changing decision and 2 actionable fixes
